statement: Before a full pilot loop, every mutating control action (pause_after, pause_now, resume, repair, cancel) should be implemented as a Temporal Update (or cancellation) with caller-generated stable cmdId used for request idempotency, and workflow-side Update handlers should immediately project the updated snapshot so Convex mirrors are timely and reliable for automation.
rationale: The current engine already has Update handlers but they do not project snapshots on Update, and Convex generates cmdIds from Date.now() which is not retry-safe. Pilot agent loops will retry commands; without idempotency and timely projection, the system will either double-apply control actions or appear stale and cause spurious repairs/resets.
keyAssumptions: Temporal TypeScript SDK supports setting a stable update request id or equivalent on workflow updates and/or using signal-with-start semantics to dedupe
Convex mirrors (process_observability + domain rows) remain the primary fast-triage surface even when Temporal UI is available
validationCriteria: Retrying the same control command (same cmdId) returns the same acknowledgement and does not create extra state transitions
After pause/resume updates, process_observability updates within a bounded SLA (e.g., <10s) without requiring stage boundaries
Agent loop can distinguish stuck execution vs stale projection
critiquePoints: Update idempotency knobs differ by SDK version; this may require a client-side de-dupe ledger instead of server-side request id
Immediate projection on Update increases write load; might be acceptable but needs bounding
questions: What is the preferred idempotency hook in @temporalio/client executeUpdate for TS 1.15.0, and how should we map cmdId onto it?
